$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0 | Copyright (c) 2025 Open Computer Use Contributors | Mount-plane file-operation RPC surface (in-guest mount client -> object-store service, guest-out over the single inspecting egress hop). STATUS: TBD. Operation NAMES and the authorization axes are sourced; per-operation request/response BODIES are not yet pinned by any field-level source and are intentionally left unspecified. JSON Schema 2020-12 (json-schema.org). NFR-SEC-25, NFR-SEC-46, NFR-SEC-51.
title: File-operation RPC surface (TBD)
description: Stub. The mount-plane file-op RPC set spoken by the in-guest mount client (the narrow object-store client + transport in one binary) to the object-store service, which translates each verb into a storage-engine request. The substrate is REST-JSON over HTTP/2: each operation is `POST <service_url>/v1/filestore/fs/<operation>` (the operation name is the path segment), the bearer rides the `Authorization` header, fileUpload carries a multipart body, fileDownload returns a chunked octet-stream; component-04 pins this route shape. Operation names and the three-axis authorization (filesystem_id + intent + downloadable) are fixed; message bodies are TBD until a field-level source pins them. Do not invent bodies — fill each operation's request/response only when sourced.

SchemaVersion.description: Interface version. Breaking change increments major (NFR-IC-04). Matches the v1alpha-style proto namespace of the file-op service.

FilesystemId.description: Per-session logical scope (the isolation unit, not a crypto boundary). The pre-issued Storage-JWT is scoped to this id; a request for a foreign filesystem_id is rejected at the storage engine with 403 PermissionDenied — a valid signature but a foreign scope; a missing or expired token is 401 — not at the egress hop (NFR-SEC-25).

Intent.description: Operation class (proto tag 1). The storage engine can deny by intent. The wire field is a proto3 string carrying no sourced enum; the read/write/preview value set below is an OCU default, not a frozen const set (mirrors the x_deny_reason hedge).

AuthorizationMetadata.description: Authorization sub-message attached to every file-op request, carried at request field 4/5. It has two members: intent (proto tag 1) and downloadable (proto tag 2; tag 9 in the upload-params carrier). The scope axis is the request's own filesystem_id sibling field (request tag 1/2, or 11 in its oneof), NOT a member of this sub-message. Richer than the bearer alone. The bearer is the pre-issued WEAK session JWT (an OCU-chosen signing algorithm, e.g. ES256) the mount client holds in its config and forwards as a static Authorization header; it is minted and signed by the Control plane (the Storage-JWT signing-key holder, which publishes a JWKS the egress edge validates against) and is NOT part of this object. ocu-filestore never accepts this weak JWT: the egress edge validates it, strips it, and exchanges it for the real filestore credential — the exchange counterparty issuing the real credential MAY be an external/customer credential authority — which is what reaches the storage engine (NFR-SEC-25).

downloadable.description: Whether data may leave the perimeter (distinct from may-read). The preview-not-download exfiltration control.

OperationName.description: The file-op RPC set, by name. Two groups: file operations and directory operations, plus chunked transfer and whole-filesystem control-plane primitives. Names are sourced; request/response bodies are TBD (see x-ocu-tbd-bodies).

DenyReason.description: Structured deny returned by the storage engine on a request it could not accept or authorize. The HTTP status is authoritative — the caller maps on status (401/403 → permission, 404 → not_found incl. anti-enumeration, 409 → already_exists, 400/422 → invalid, 429/503 → retryable); this body is diagnostic, never the authority. It carries no internal topology (NFR-SEC-51, NFR-SEC-17): a stable reason_code and a bounded message, no stack trace, no backend prefix, no resource path. Same BoundedReason envelope as the exec channel (contracts/exec/exec-channel.schema.json#/$defs/BoundedReason) and the control-RPC endpoint (contracts/control/control-rpc.schema.json#/$defs/BoundedReason) — one envelope across all three OCU machine surfaces (NFR-SEC-51). The reason_code vocabulary below is the OCU default value set for the storage surface; it is an OCU default, not a sourced const set, so an engine may add a code as an additive change — but every code matches the frozen pattern and the envelope shape is frozen.

reason_code.x-ocu-default-deny-vocab: OCU default value set; additive, the pattern and envelope are frozen

message.$comment: x-ocu-default: maxLength 256 is the NFR-SEC-51 default, retunable per workload tier. Matches the exec-channel and control-RPC BoundedReason.

ReadFileRequest_Range.description: Optional ranged-read sub-message on a readFile request. Half-open byte range [offset, offset+length). The sub-message and its offset/length fields are sourced; absence of the sub-message means a whole-object read.

length.description: Byte count from offset; the range is [offset, offset+length). A single ranged read is bounded by the RPC message ceiling (see SizeLimits.rpc_message_ceiling_bytes); larger reads stream in the default read-chunk size.

ListDirectoryRecursiveCursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor carried across pages of a recursive listDirectory. Sourced as an opaque token; the object-store service mints and validates it, the mount client echoes it unmodified. Absent on the first page; the object-store service omits it on the last page.
ListDirectoryRecursiveCursor.x-ocu-tbd: Cursor wire ENCODING (what the opaque token packs) is not pinned; the opaque-string contract is sourced, the internal structure stays TBD. Do not model decoded subfields.

FileUploadRequest_Params.description: Chunked-upload params sub-message on a fileUpload request. The object-store service reassembles the streamed chunks; this is NOT a single inline body. Chunking is mandatory because a single RPC message is capped (see SizeLimits.rpc_message_ceiling_bytes), independent of the engine max-file-size and the per-mount VFS cache ceiling.

chunk.description: One chunk of the upload byte stream. The object-store service reassembles chunks in order into the target object. A chunk's encoded size stays under the RPC message ceiling.

numChunks.description: Total chunk count the object-store service expects before reassembly completes.

SizeLimits.description: Layered transfer size limits. Each layer is independent. The RPC ceiling forces chunking; the backend max-file-size is a deployment policy enforced at the storage engine; the VFS cache ceiling is per-mount; the read-chunk default sizes streamed reads. NFR-SEC-46.

rpc_message_ceiling_bytes.description: Per-RPC-message ceiling. A transfer above it MUST be chunked (fileUpload via FileUploadRequest_Params; ranged readFile via ReadFileRequest_Range). NFR-SEC-46.
rpc_message_ceiling_bytes.x-ocu-default: ~4 MiB per-message ceiling that forces chunking on the REST file-op leg; configurable, not a frozen protocol constant.

read_chunk_default_bytes.description: Default read-chunk size for streamed reads.
read_chunk_default_bytes.x-ocu-default: 128 MiB default read-chunk size; configurable per workload tier.

broker_max_file_size_bytes.description: Maximum object size enforced at the storage engine. A deployment policy figure, not fixed in this contract. (Field name retained for wire compatibility.)
broker_max_file_size_bytes.x-ocu-tbd: Server-side figure, not fixed in the contract. No const.

vfs_cache_max_size_bytes.description: Per-mount local VFS cache ceiling. Authoritative field is mount-config.schema.json vfs_cache_max_size; mirrored here as the read-path reference. Objects above it stream rather than cache.
vfs_cache_max_size_bytes.x-ocu-default: 1 GiB per-mount cache ceiling; canonical owner is mount-config vfs_cache_max_size.

x-ocu-tbd-bodies.reason: The sourced message shapes (ReadFileRequest_Range, ListDirectoryRecursiveCursor, FileUploadRequest_Params) and the layered SizeLimits are now real $defs, reachable from the request envelope. What stays TBD is the wire field set inside the remaining request bodies (createFile, readMetadata) and every response body. Fill each only when a field-level source pins it.

known-shape-hints.getFileMetadata: single-object metadata read by uuid; response field set TBD (distinct from listFiles, the directory-enumeration op, and from readFileMetadata, the path+filesystem_id read)
known-shape-hints.readFileMetadata: metadata read by path + filesystem_id; request/response field set TBD (distinct from readMetadata, the path-addressed dir-entry read, and getFileMetadata, the by-uuid read)
known-shape-hints.listFiles: object enumeration under a scope; response entry shape TBD (distinct from getFileMetadata)
known-shape-hints.fileDownload: mount-plane read-path counterpart to the chunked fileUpload; streams the object back, the object-store service chunks it under the RPC message ceiling; request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.importFiles: session-pseudobucket import; request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.importZip: one-op zip import (the whole archive imported in a single op); request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.migrateFilesystem: in-session pseudobucket control primitive (rebind the session scope, not a durable-store data migration); request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.removeFilesystem: session-pseudobucket control primitive; request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.fileDelete: delete-by-uuid (distinct from removeFile, the path-addressed delete); request/response field set TBD
known-shape-hints.releaseQuarantinedFiles: whole-filesystem-scope quarantine-release control op, grouped with migrateFilesystem / removeFilesystem; request carries verdictRef + fsId + sourceOrg + targetOrg, response carries filesReleased; field set TBD

read_range.description: Present only on op=readFile; optional ranged read, absent => whole-object read. The carrier name is design-level; the ReadFileRequest_Range shape it references is sourced.
read_range.x-ocu-design: envelope carrier name is design-level; the referenced sub-message shape is sourced.

recursive_cursor.description: Present only on a recursive op=listDirectory continuation; the opaque page cursor. The carrier name is design-level; the ListDirectoryRecursiveCursor shape it references is sourced.
recursive_cursor.x-ocu-design: envelope carrier name is design-level; the referenced sub-message shape is sourced.

upload_params.description: Present only on op=fileUpload; the chunked-upload params (mandatory for fileUpload — there is no single-inline-body form). The carrier name is design-level; the FileUploadRequest_Params shape it references is sourced.
upload_params.x-ocu-design: envelope carrier name is design-level; the referenced sub-message shape is sourced.

size_limits.description: Read-only reference to the layered transfer ceilings that bound this request (RPC message ceiling, read-chunk default, backend max-file-size, per-mount cache ceiling). Carrier name is design-level; the SizeLimits shape it references is sourced. Op-gated carriers (read_range / recursive_cursor / upload_params) apply only to their named op.
size_limits.x-ocu-design: envelope carrier name is design-level; the referenced shape is sourced.
